content:
How a simple enhancement to Kivex React reminded me that good software is never finished. It keeps evolving.

3 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

When I first started contributing to the Kivex ecosystem, I thought the biggest challenge would be building the packages.
I was wrong.
Building Kivex React was only the beginning.
After that came Kivex Solid, where I spent hours debugging a problem that turned out to be the build tool instead of my code. That experience taught me to question not only my implementation but also the tools behind it.
Next came Kivex Svelte. Thanks to everything I had learned from the previous two packages, I completed it in less than 24 hours.
I thought I was done.
I wasn’t.
Recently, while reviewing Kivex React, we discovered something important.
The icon components supported the custom props we had designed, but they did not forward the standard SVG properties that React developers expect to use every day.
That meant developers couldn’t easily pass properties like className, style, id, aria-*, data-*, or other native SVG attributes directly to the icons.
